race.name: Haanja Maraton
race.tagline: 42 kilometers of classic skiing through the rolling hills of southern Estonia — where the Baltic's highest point meets one of the country's oldest marathon traditions.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations with warm drinks, porridge, and energy food

climate.description: Late January in southern Estonia brings cold continental conditions. Temperatures typically range from -15°C to -3°C. The Haanja Uplands receive reliable snowfall and cold temperatures that preserve snow well. The rolling terrain creates wind-exposed hilltops and sheltered valleys.
challenges: Cold Baltic continental conditions with wind chill on hilltops
Rolling terrain means constant climbing and descending
Classic technique on hilly terrain requires strong diagonal stride
Variable snow conditions between exposed hilltops and sheltered forests

course.primary: Loop course through the Haanja Uplands in southern Estonia, home to the Baltic states' highest point (Suur Munamägi, 318m). Despite the modest altitude, the terrain is surprisingly hilly with constant rolling elevation changes. The course winds through mixed forest and open farmland in a landscape shaped by glacial moraines.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through the Haanja Uplands
course.grooming: Good machine grooming by Estoloppet series organizers
features: Set in the Haanja Uplands — Baltic states' highest terrain
Part of the Estoloppet series — Estonia's national marathon series
Rolling glacial moraine landscape with constant elevation changes
Suur Munamägi (318m), the Baltic states' highest point, is nearby
Southern Estonian countryside — forest, farms, and frozen lakes

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A solid Baltic marathon ski event on surprisingly hilly terrain. Part of the Estoloppet series gives it national significance. Estonia's Nordic skiing tradition is genuine but the international profile is modest. Remote southern Estonian location limits accessibility.

history.summary: The Haanja Maraton is the second-oldest marathon ski race in Estonia and a key event in the Estoloppet series — Estonia's national citizen marathon ski series. Set in the Haanja Uplands of southern Estonia, the race takes place in the hilliest terrain in the Baltic states. Estonia has a deep Nordic skiing tradition, with cross-country skiing serving as both a national sport and a practical winter transportation method for centuries in rural areas.
notable_facts: Second-oldest marathon ski race in Estonia
Part of the Estoloppet — Estonia's national marathon ski series
Set in the Haanja Uplands, the Baltic states' highest terrain
Suur Munamägi (318m), the Baltic states' highest point, is nearby
Estonia has one of the strongest Nordic skiing traditions in the Baltic region
Classic-only format reflects Estonian skiing tradition
